test.observation: All tests passed (exit code 0), including heap, slab, buddy, vma, heap_routing, oom, and all other test binaries. Warnings present (dead_code in paging.rs constants, unused fields in cross_module.rs) but no errors.

build.observation: Finished release profile [optimized] in 0.00s — no errors, no warnings

audit-heap.summary: Documentation-only change (18 lines). AUDIT comment correctly enumerates all 7 unsafe constructs, verifies dealloc routing invariant (address-based, not size-based), and documents OOM handling. Independent review confirms no bugs. Existing test coverage adequate (heap.rs + heap_routing.rs).

audit-slab.summary: Documentation-only change (7 lines). AUDIT comment correctly identifies 2 unsafe blocks + 2 unsafe fn, applies 6-category checklist. No bugs found (confirmed independently). One pre-existing SAFETY comment inaccuracy (FreeNode size stated as 16, actually 8) — no correctness impact.

issues.description: Pre-existing SAFETY comment in free() states FreeNode is 16 bytes on 64-bit, actually 8 bytes (single pointer). Safety invariant still holds (min class 64 >= 8). No correctness impact.

audit-page-allocator.summary: AUDIT comment (5 lines) + PA validation model test (44 lines). All 6 unsafe blocks + 1 unsafe impl verified. Model test correctly mirrors kernel's free_frames() validation with matching constants from paging.rs. Comprehensive coverage of misaligned, below-range, above-range, valid addresses, and boundaries.

audit-memory-region.summary: AUDIT comment + 8 new edge case tests. Pure logic audit (0 unsafe). Binary search operations verified for empty list, adjacent VMAs, gaps, boundary addresses, zero-length/inverted ranges. 3 call sites enforcing no-overlaps invariant verified. Combined test coverage: 29 pre-existing + 8 new + 7 adversarial.

issues.description: Test comment says 'Smallest valid VMA: one byte' but kernel VMAs are page-aligned, so smallest real VMA is one page. Test logic is correct, comment is slightly misleading.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: In the kernel-auditor skill, make the 'no bugs found' path more explicit. Currently Step 5 says 'Write Failing Tests First (TDD) for EVERY code bug finding' and the Special Cases section says 'If no bugs are found'. Workers have to reason about whether Steps 5-6 apply when the audit finds no bugs. Suggest adding: 'If no bugs are found, skip Steps 5-6 entirely and proceed to Step 7 (AUDIT marker).'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: audit-heap worker's skill feedback noted this ambiguity. The worker correctly deduced the right behavior but had to reason through the applicability. Making this explicit would reduce cognitive overhead for future audit workers.
